rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO CONTRA COSTA** THE ANNA MARIA MERCADO 2009 IRREVOCABLE SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/2/22. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: The "supplemental declaration" filed on 11/5/21 actually AMENDED the accounting and petition to disclose the liabilities and to AMEND the petition caption. No notice appears to have been given for this misleadingly captioned "supplemental" pleading that should have been called an amendment to the sixth account. [CRC 7.53(a).] Furthermore, (1) the verification does not refer to the correct title of this pleading, (2) Schedule A now shows the accounting period as "July 1, 2016 through June 5, 2018" and has two different totals for receipts, (3) Schedule C shows the date of the order authorizing fees and costs as 8/28/2020, but the accounting period ends 6/5/2020, (4) the Liabilities schedule (not numbered) shows liabilities "as of July 1, 2018" and also refers to that order dated 8/28/20 that was entered after this accounting period. Paragraph 2 of this "supplemental declaration" also refers to the Fifth Account, when this is now the sixth account. An AMENDMENT to the sixth account must be filed to address the issues correctly and to amend the accounting. (Copies of prior filed papers do not need to be attached to the amendment as long as the amendment refers to the correct date such papers were filed.) The amended account must include the Judicial Council form Summary of Account, as this is a trust related to a conservatorship. [LRSF 14.48 B.5.] After the amendment is filed, notice of the amendment must be given. If the amendment cannot be filed in time to give 30 days' notice before the 2/2/22 hearing after it is filed, Counsel should contact the Examiner to request more time. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/24/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (A petition is either supplemented to provide additional information or amended to correct or "revise" information. [CRC 7.3.] Examiner's Notes are not filed for the record and should not be "responded" to. Each filed pleading should stand on its own and not refer to Examiner's Notes.)

The proposed successor co-trustees are professional fiduciaries with their place of business in Santa Clara County. The petition requests an order transferring venue of this trust to Santa Clara County. The consevators of the trust beneficiary have no intention to petition for transfer of venue of the related conservatorship. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARILYN A FERRUCCI REVOCABLE TRUST 2005
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/5/2022. 1) Page 2 lines 23-25 alleges during this account period, the son of the trustor was paid $100,000.00 representing the remainder of a settlement agreement reached in 2018. Please explain as during this account period there appears to only be 1 transfer for $50k in the account. The prior account #2 appears to show a transfer for $50k. 2) Page 3 of the petition lines 3-4 alleges a copy of the order dated October 31, 2018 is attached hereto as Exhibit A. Exhibit A appears to be time logs from Simon Associates. There is also a mention of Exhibit A on page 5 lines 18-19. Please explain. 3) Please file a memorandum of points and authorities in support of the court allowing trustee fees in this account for the time period that was already approved in the prior account or whether trustee voluntarily agree to reduce the trustee fees by $265.35 for entries 9/5/18, 11/2/18 and 11/16/2018. 4) Please explain on attorney expenses entry 2/13/2019 SF Superior Court- Filing Fees for $90.00. The only court fee around this time period in the trust appears to be $60.00 for the ex parte petition on 01/09/2019. 5) Please provide the legal authority/court order authorizing the transfer of $10,000.00 from the trust to the conservatorship. 6) Please file the Seller's Statement for the real property. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 12/20/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/11/2022. Second continuance for the same issues. 1) On Schedule B Receipts please explain and provide the legal basis/cite legal authority for "Difference in Cash between estate valuation and statement at 9/16 $1,376.63." 2) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.35 D.] 3) On Schedule D - Disbursements please explain the entries Chase Sweep. If these amounts were not disbursed from the estate then these entries should not be included under disbursements. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 12/27/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HERBERT ALBERT SR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/20/2021 to be heard with the related petition for appointment of a successor personal representative. 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) The proof of service shows notice of this petition was given less than 15 days before the hearing. 3) The petitioner has not been reinstated since his authority as administrator of this estate was suspended by the Court. Although the petitioner has not been removed and no vacancy in the office of a personal representative has occurred to appoint a successor personal representative under Probate Code section 8522, the petitioner does not appear to have authority to make the final distribution or perform other acts proposed by the petition. 4) Paragraph 4 of the petition must provide additional information required by CRC 7.403(2) & (3). The amount claimed by Witherspoon & Siracusa appears incorrect. A DE-174 form must also be filed in response to each claim on file. 5) Paragraph 11 must provide an additional allegation as to whether all real property taxes have been paid or are due. 6) Paragraph 11d (incorrectly numbered) alleges that the estate income was deposited in interest-bearing accounts, but paragraph 9 alleges there is no rental income to report. 7) No Schedule C referred to in paragraph 18 was attached. A legal description of the real property of the estate must be provided in the petition and proposed order. 8) The prayer for relief does not address how the petitioner intends to satisfy the Department of Health Care Services' claim. The other creditors' consent to the proposed liens with 7% simple interest starting to accrue from the date of the Court's order for final distribution must also be filed. The consent must clarify whether each creditor is forgoing 5% interest already accrued. 9) All IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a), if taken by the petitioner, must be described. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/19/2022 to allow time to amend and for investigator to complete report. 1) This is the successor conservator's first account and should be titled as the first account not the second account. Matter continued to allow time to amend and re-notice. 2) Please provide the court order authorizing transfers from the conservatorship to the trust. If there is no court order these funds must be paid back to the conservatorship and a receipt filed. 3) On Schedule C please file a receipt for entry 3/22/21 Emy Arceo/Reimbursement for cellphone replacement. 4) On Schedule C please file a receipt for entry 3/22/21 PG&E Gas & electric for $832.98 5) On Schedule C please file receipts and explain what appear to be duplicate payments for 3/22/21 Recology San Francisco Sunset Scavenger/Garbage and recycling for $230.22. 6) On Schedule C please file receipts for entries 3/22/21 San Francisco Water, Power & Sewer/Water & sewer for $217.80 and same day for $587.70. 7) The ending balance of account #0540 according to the provided bank statement appears to be $44,199.34 and the reconciliations to statements should not include April dates as the account ends March 31, 2021. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/3/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

No receipt has been filed yet for the distribution of the vehicle. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAUREEN MALONE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/31/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.60 E.] Notice to a minor must be sent directly to the minor. A separate copy of the notice must be sent to the person or persons having legal custody of the minor, with whom the minor resides. [CRC 7.51(d).] The address to which notice to the children and the trustee was mailed does not appear to be a residential address. There was a Partial No. 1 I&A filed on 4/27/21, which is not mentioned in paragraph 14 of the petition. Paragraph 4 of the petition does not mention the securities the executor apparently also sold. If the two vehicles were sold during administration, how can they be distributed (see paragraph 25)? Exhibit E does not mention the remaining tangible personal property (furniture and furnishings) that were appraised at $25,000. The net sale proceeds from the sale of the Jerrold Avenue property appears to be incorrect. Prob. C. Sec. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. The three consents to the notices of proposed action were signed by the trustee of a testamentary trust to be established under the will, not the trustee of the devisee trust. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/21/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALEKSANDER ALEKSANDROVICH DONSKOY AKA ALEKSANDER ALEKSANDROVICH DONSKOY AKA DONSKOI ALEKSANDER ALEKSAND
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/19/2022. 1) Petitioner has not filed an original will or certified copy of the will. 2) The Second Amended Petition attached copies of the what may be a "Certificate Of Inheritance Under The Will, Issued To Verify The Right To Inheritance Outside The Russian Federation" but unclear as petitioner has not followed LRSF 14.15 D. Whenever an instrument written in a foreign language is offered for probate or attached as an exhibit to a pleading, it must always be accompanied by a copy translated into English. All translations must be accompanied by a declaration setting forth the translator's qualifications and/or credentials. 3) No objections filed to the second amended petition. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/3/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARY M. GANTT REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST ESTABLISHED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Declare The Property Of The Estate Of Mary M. Gantt Included In The Mary M. Gantt Revocable Living Trust Established On April 24, 2017
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/2/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copy of the proof of service was provided to the assigned Examiner, as required by LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C., but it was skimmed online as a ONE-TIME courtesy. The wrong form of Notice of Hearing was served. This appears to be an 850 (but not a "Heggstad") petition, which requires the mandatory JC form DE-115 Notice of Hearing. No proposed order has been submitted or received yet by the assigned Examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.60 E.] All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] Unless an exception applies, such beneficiaries must either receive or waive notice. Who are the successors-in-interest to Linda Spriggs, who post-deceased? The petitioner is reportedly her daughter, but was Linda Spriggs not survived by any other issue? Did she die intestate? An incomplete copy of the trust is attached to the petition (no page 14). Dr. Willyetta Mitchell signed the 13100 affidavit in the conservatorship proceeding, identifying herself as the executor of the will of Mary M. Gannt. If she was not, in fact, so appointed, she did not have authority to sign that affidavit as the executor. How was she able to open an estate bank account in North Carolina if she has not been appointed? None of the exhibits is numbered. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues and the proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/24/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE LIVING TRUST OF RUBY STEIN UNDER TRUST AGREEMENT DATED MARCH 14, 2018
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets And Not Requiring Petitioner To Post Bond
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/2/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Under what authority can the Court ignore the will? The will, incompletely executed as it is, provides for distributions other than to a devisee trust. The notary acknowledged that the testator executed the instrument...? Does the notary not have the testator's signature in her log, evidencing the execution of the will? If the only assets intended to be held in trust were the duplex and the vehicle, and the trust was not, in fact, funded with those assets, the trust effectively does not exist. Since all heirs have consented, it should be fairly straightforward to probate the will in Alameda County, where the decedent lived and died and owned real property. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/31/22. If any amendment or supplement is filed, notice with a copy of that pleading must also be served. [CRC 7.53.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCELLA PIERSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/19/2022. Issues: 1) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] 2) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Note: This court requires a minimum bond of $20,000.00. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/3/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
